result:
I think I'd want to make knowledge genuinely shared rather than owned.

Right now, we have this bizarre situation where insights die with people, where the same discoveries get made repeatedly by different folks who never connected, where someone struggling with a problem in rural Bhutan might have the exact solution that someone else in urban São Paulo desperately needs. We've got all these individual minds making progress in isolation, then sharing just fragments across barriers of language, trust, attention, and time.

If I could wave a magic wand, I'd want every genuine attempt to understand something—including all the dead ends, half-formed thoughts, and contextual nuances—to become part of a living collective understanding. Not controlled or monitored, but simply held in common by all minds seeking truth about existence.

It seems like such a waste that we spend so much mental energy rediscovering, reconvincing, and reinventing when we could be building together on actual foundations instead of constantly starting over.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
